---
title: "U-Net: Last Layer - 2ch"
---

::: {.callout-note appearance="default" collapse="false"}
Q: Focus on this 1x1 conv. How does it create 2 channels. Please explain with diagram.
:::


> **A 1Г—1 convolution creates multiple channels by applying multiple independent dot-products to the feature vector at each pixel.**


letвҖҷs zoom **only** into the **1Г—1 convolution** and explain **exactly how it creates 2 channels**, step by step, with diagrams.

---

## 1. What goes *into* the 1Г—1 convolution?

From the last decoder block of U-Net:

```
Feature map: 388 Г— 388 Г— 64
```

At **each pixel location (i, j)** you have a **64-dimensional feature vector**:

```
f(i,j) = [f1, f2, f3, ..., f64]
```

Think of this as:

> вҖңEverything the network knows about this pixel.вҖқ

---

## 2. What does вҖң1Г—1 convolution with 2 output channelsвҖқ mean?

In PyTorch:

```python
nn.Conv2d(
    in_channels=64,
    out_channels=2,
    kernel_size=1
)
```

This creates:

* **2 separate filters**
* Each filter is size **1Г—1Г—64**
* Each filter produces **1 output channel**

So:

```
2 filters  вҶ’  2 output channels
```

---

## 3. Structure of the filters (very important)

### Filter for class 0 (e.g. background)

```
W_bg: shape (64)
b_bg: scalar bias
```

### Filter for class 1 (e.g. foreground)

```
W_fg: shape (64)
b_fg: scalar bias
```

Visually:

```
1Г—1 conv weights:

Filter 0 (background):
[w1_bg, w2_bg, ..., w64_bg] + b_bg

Filter 1 (foreground):
[w1_fg, w2_fg, ..., w64_fg] + b_fg
```

---

## 4. What happens at **one pixel** (core idea)

At pixel (i, j):

```
Input feature vector:
[f1, f2, ..., f64]
```

Each filter does a **dot product**.

### Background channel

```
score_bg(i,j) =
f1В·w1_bg + f2В·w2_bg + ... + f64В·w64_bg + b_bg
```

### Foreground channel

```
score_fg(i,j) =
f1В·w1_fg + f2В·w2_fg + ... + f64В·w64_fg + b_fg
```

---

## 5. Diagram: single pixel view (most intuitive)

```
           64 feature values
        в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
        в”Ӯ f1  f2  ...  f64    в”Ӯ
        в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
              в”Ӯ
      в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
      в”Ӯ                в”Ӯ
  Filter BG         Filter FG
 (1Г—1Г—64)         (1Г—1Г—64)
      в”Ӯ                в”Ӯ
      в–ј                в–ј
 score_bg          score_fg
```

**Result at that pixel:**

```
[ score_bg , score_fg ]
```

---

## 6. Diagram: full image view

Apply the same operation **independently at every pixel**:

```
Input feature map
388Г—388Г—64
      в”Ӯ
      в–ј
1Г—1 conv (2 filters)
      в”Ӯ
      в–ј
Output logits
388Г—388Г—2
```

Expanded:

```
Each pixel:
[64 features] вҶ’ [2 scores]
```

---

## 7. Why this is equivalent to a per-pixel linear classifier

At each pixel:

```
logits = W В· f + b
```

Where:

* `W` = 2 Г— 64 matrix
* `f` = 64 Г— 1 vector
* `b` = 2 Г— 1 bias

This is **exactly logistic / softmax regression per pixel**.

---

## 8. Why spatial information is NOT mixed here

Because kernel size = **1Г—1**:

* No neighboring pixels are seen
* No spatial smoothing
* Decision is purely based on **features already learned**

ThatвҖҷs why:

> All spatial reasoning must happen **before** this layer.

---

## 9. What the 2 channels represent (important clarity)

The output **channels are not masks yet**.

They are:

```
Channel 0 вҶ’ background LOGIT
Channel 1 вҶ’ foreground LOGIT
```

Only after:

```
Softmax(channel dimension)
```

do they become probabilities.
